steps.title: Recognize two independent clauses in contrast
steps.content:
The sentence contains two complete thoughts: (1) scientists previously believed the Sun was in a quiet phase at that [time], and (2) the Lund team's finding suggests otherwise. Because "suggests otherwise" directly contradicts the previous belief, the two clauses are in contrast and must be joined correctly.
  • Choice A — 'time but': Uses the contrasting conjunction "but" without the required comma before it when joining two longer main clauses. ✗
  • Choice B — 'time, but': A comma followed by the coordinating conjunction "but" correctly joins two independent clauses and signals the contrast. ✓
  • Choice C — 'time,': A comma alone joining two independent clauses creates a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice D — 'time': No punctuation or conjunction between the two main clauses creates a run-on sentence. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the match
steps.content: Only "time, but" uses both the required comma and a coordinating conjunction to properly join the two contrasting independent clauses. That matches choice B — B is correct.
